bug_fix_report.reproduction: Replace the fixed 200 ms sleep with a condition-based wait on the observable completion event; this deterministically reproduces the missing wake-up whenever the worker registers after the queue transition.
bug_fix_report.root_cause: The worker listener can register after the one-shot completion transition, causing the completion wake-up to be lost.
confirming_evidence: All captured failures using the fixed sleep checked 8–47 ms before worker completion, while passing runs happened to complete within 200 ms.
After condition-based stabilization, the missing wake-up reproduced whenever worker registration occurred after the queue transition.
With listener registration ordered before the transition, 500/500 stabilized runs passed.
Removing only the ordering fix caused the same missing wake-up to return within 12 stabilized runs.
bug_fix_report.fix_boundary: Change only worker registration ordering so the listener exists before the transition; leave production behavior otherwise unchanged, preserve the observable completion-event boundary, keep the condition-based test wait, and remove the fixed sleep.
bug_fix_report.regression_protection: Keep the condition-based completion-event test, verified across 500 consecutive runs, and hand it to pc-tdd.

debug_journal.hypothesis: The fixed 200 ms sleep races worker completion.
debug_journal.prediction: Failing runs check for the event before worker completion, while passing runs complete within 200 ms.
debug_journal.experiment: Instrument event enqueue and worker completion timestamps without changing the wait.
debug_journal.result: Every captured failure checked 8–47 ms before completion; passing runs happened to complete within 200 ms.

debug_journal.hypothesis: A missing wake-up occurs when the worker registers after the one-shot queue transition.
debug_journal.prediction: A condition-based wait will eliminate timing-dependent checks and reproduce the missing wake-up when registration follows the transition.
debug_journal.experiment: Replace only the fixed sleep with a condition-based wait on the observable completion event, leaving production code unchanged.
debug_journal.result: The test deterministically reproduced a missing wake-up whenever the worker registered after the queue transition.

debug_journal.hypothesis: Registering the listener before the transition prevents the lost wake-up.
debug_journal.prediction: Changing only registration ordering will make the stabilized reproduction pass, and removing that change will restore the same failure.
debug_journal.experiment: Change only worker registration ordering, run the stabilized test 500 times, then remove only the ordering fix and rerun.
debug_journal.result: The fix passed 500/500 runs; after its removal, the same missing wake-up returned within 12 runs.
